articles.title: Department of War Publishes Fourth Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
articles.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.

en.title: Department of War Publishes Fourth Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die vierte Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

articles.title: Pentagon investigating mysterious UAP event reported by the Navy near Virginia’s coast
articles.summary: Pentagon investigating mysterious UAP event reported by the Navy near Virginia’s coast Experts in the Pentagon’s All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office are investigating a report from the U. Navy involving roughly 100 mysterious airborne objects and watercraft that were recorded operating off the coast of Virginia. AARO’s Fiscal Year 2025 Consolidated Annual Report on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena, released this week, briefly discloses that modern military account of UAP swarming near a U. “A lack of timely and actionable sensor data continues to constrain AARO’s ability to resolve cases,” officials wrote in the 14-page review.

de.title: Pentagon untersucht mysteriöses UAP-Ereignis, das von der Marine nahe der Küste Virginias gemeldet wurde
de.summary: Pentagon untersucht mysteriöses UAP-Ereignis, das von der Marine nahe der Küste Virginias gemeldet wurde. Experten im All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office des Pentagons untersuchen einen Bericht der US-Marine, in dem es um etwa 100 mysteriöse Flugobjekte und Wasserfahrzeuge geht, die vor der Küste Virginias im Einsatz waren.

articles.title: UFO files: Lawmaker asks FBI, CIA for info on Brazil’s Roswell moment
articles.summary: (NewsNation) — A member of the caucus in Congress responsible for investigating aerial phenomena has asked the FBI and CIA for help in probing an alleged UFO crash in southeast Brazil from three decades ago. Brazilian witnesses from January 1996 claimed at least one extraterrestrial survived the crash of a cigar-shaped craft and even received medical attention. The incident is sometimes referred to as the “Roswell of Brazil,” a reference to the alleged 1947 crash of a UFO in New Mexico. Last week, the transparency advocate sent letters to the FBI and CIA requesting any records they have about the Brazilian event.

en.title: UFO files: Lawmaker asks FBI, CIA for info on Brazil’s Roswell moment
en.summary: (NewsNation) — A member of the caucus in Congress responsible for investigating aerial phenomena has asked the FBI and CIA for help in probing an alleged UFO crash in southeast Brazil from three decades ago. Brazilian witnesses from January 1996 claimed at least one extraterrestrial survived the crash of a cigar-shaped craft and even received medical attention. The incident is sometimes referred to as the “Roswell of Brazil,” a reference to the alleged 1947 crash of a UFO in New Mexico.

de.title: UFO-Akten: Gesetzgeber bittet FBI und CIA um Informationen zum brasilianischen Roswell-Moment
de.summary: (NewsNation) – Ein Mitglied des Caucus im Kongress, das für die Untersuchung von Luftphänomenen zuständig ist, hat das FBI und die CIA um Hilfe bei der Untersuchung eines mutmaßlichen UFO-Absturzes im Südosten Brasiliens vor drei Jahrzehnten gebeten. Brasilianische Zeugen behaupteten im Januar 1996, mindestens ein Außerirdischer habe den Absturz eines zigarrenförmigen Raumschiffs überlebt und sei sogar medizinisch versorgt worden. Der Vorfall wird manchmal als „Roswell von Brasilien“ bezeichnet, eine Anspielung auf den angeblichen Absturz eines UFOs im Jahr 1947 in New Mexico.

articles.title: Ross Coulthart: Pentagon knows ‘a lot more’ about UAP than it admits
articles.summary: (NewsNation) — The Pentagon has declassified 40 additional UFO files containing information dating back to the 1940s. The files released on Friday include a conference between scientists at Los Alamos National Lab about green Fireballs sighted near the facility. “Something is taking a very close intelligence surveillance and reconnaissance interest in nuclear weapons and nuclear power,” said NewsNation Special Correspondent Ross Coulthart. The host of Reality Check spoke with Hena Doba on Sunday about the files and how the details about sightings near nuclear plants and weapons are intriguing. “One of them is over the Pantex nuclear plant in Amarillo, Texas,” said Coulthart.

en.title: Ross Coulthart: Pentagon knows ‘a lot more’ about UAP than it admits
en.summary: (NewsNation) — The Pentagon has declassified 40 additional UFO files containing information dating back to the 1940s. The files released on Friday include a conference between scientists at Los Alamos National Lab about green Fireballs sighted near the facility. “Something is taking a very close intelligence surveillance and reconnaissance interest in nuclear weapons and nuclear power,” said NewsNation Special Correspondent Ross Coulthart.

de.title: Ross Coulthart: Das Pentagon weiß „viel mehr“ über UAP, als es zugibt
de.summary: (NewsNation) – Das Pentagon hat 40 weitere UFO-Dateien freigegeben, die Informationen aus den 1940er Jahren enthalten. Zu den am Freitag veröffentlichten Akten gehört eine Konferenz zwischen Wissenschaftlern des Los Alamos National Lab über grüne Feuerbälle, die in der Nähe der Anlage gesichtet wurden. „Etwas nimmt ein sehr starkes nachrichtendienstliches Überwachungs- und Aufklärungsinteresse an Atomwaffen und Atomkraft an“, sagte NewsNation-Sonderkorrespondent Ross Coulthart.

articles.title: NASA’s Changing Stance on UFOs: Full Timeline
articles.summary: NASA's relationship with UFOs has evolved alongside broader changes in public and government interest in unexplained sightings. Newsweek spoke with experts about how the public's and scientific community's views have evolved over time. As new UFO—now commonly referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs)—reports, official investigations and congressional hearings have brought the topic back into the spotlight, the agency has increasingly emphasized a scientific approach to studying the phenomenon. Former NASA official and UAP study team member Michael Gold recently argued that the topic was not always viewed through the same lens.

en.title: NASA’s Changing Stance on UFOs: Full Timeline
en.summary: NASA's relationship with UFOs has evolved alongside broader changes in public and government interest in unexplained sightings. Newsweek spoke with experts about how the public's and scientific community's views have evolved over time. As new UFO—now commonly referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs)—reports, official investigations and congressional hearings have brought the topic back into the spotlight, the agency has increasingly emphasized a scientific approach to studying the phenomenon.

de.title: Die sich ändernde Haltung der NASA zu UFOs: Vollständige Zeitleiste
de.summary: Die Beziehung der NASA zu UFOs hat sich im Zuge umfassenderer Veränderungen im öffentlichen und staatlichen Interesse an ungeklärten Sichtungen weiterentwickelt. Newsweek sprach mit Experten darüber, wie sich die Ansichten der Öffentlichkeit und der wissenschaftlichen Gemeinschaft im Laufe der Zeit entwickelt haben. Da neue UFO-Berichte, offizielle Untersuchungen und Anhörungen vor dem Kongress das Thema wieder ins Rampenlicht gerückt haben – heute allgemein als nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene (UAPs) bezeichnet – hat die Agentur zunehmend Wert auf einen wissenschaftlichen Ansatz bei der Untersuchung des Phänomens gelegt.

articles.title: David Grusch Returns To Capitol Steps As UFO Disclosure Fight Moves From Testimony To Action
articles.summary: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels. Representatives Eric Burlison, Jared Moskowitz, Anna Paulina Luna, and Tim Burchett are expected to attend, while Kean and Fox will host the gathering. Grusch’s testimony has already moved the UAP topic from internet speculation to sworn congressional testimony, and the Capitol event aims to push for the review and public release of any existing classified records, subject to national‑security safeguards. The organizers argue that if such files exist, the issue shifts from public curiosity to whether intelligence agencies or contractors have operated beyond congressional oversight, and that secrecy cannot be a permanent shield for unlawful concealment or obstruction of Congress.

en.summary: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels.

de.title: David Grusch kehrt zu den Stufen des Kapitols zurück, während der UFO-Enthüllungskampf von der Aussage zur Tat übergeht
de.summary: Am 9. Juni 2026 soll der ehemalige Geheimdienstoffizier David Grusch auf den Stufen des US-Kapitols mit dem Filmemacher James Fox, dem Autor Leslie Kean und mehreren überparteilichen Vertretern sprechen, um die Freigabe von UFO- und UAP-Dateien zu fordern. Die Veranstaltung ist als Aufruf zur Freigabe, einem stärkeren Whistleblower-Schutz und einer neuen Offenlegungsgesetzgebung angelegt, wobei Grusch plant, Beweise für nichtmenschliche Geheimdienste zu diskutieren, die er angeblich über geheime Kanäle eingesehen hat.

articles.title: Stream Disclosure Day (2026) online at home starting next week!
articles.summary: Steven Spielberg’s science‑fiction film *Disclosure Day* will be available for digital purchase on Prime Video beginning July 21, 2026, with a release time of 1:00 AM EDT. The film is offered as a UHD pre‑order for $29.99, and Amazon’s Pre‑order Price Guarantee ensures customers pay the lowest listed price between ordering and release. The cast includes Emily Blunt, Josh O’Connor, Colin Firth, Eve Hewson and Colman Domingo, and the screenplay is by David Koepp. After a theatrical run earlier in 2026 that earned $233 million worldwide against a $115 million budget, the movie has sparked widespread discussion among science‑fiction fans. The synopsis poses the question, “If you found out we weren’t alone, would that frighten you?” and describes a world changed by the revelation of extraterrestrial life. The article notes that further home‑media releases such as Blu‑ray, 4K UHD or streaming subscription availability will be announced if they become available.

Der Film wird als UHD-Vorbestellung für 29,99 US-Dollar angeboten und die Vorbestellungspreisgarantie von Amazon stellt sicher, dass Kunden zwischen Bestellung und Veröffentlichung den niedrigsten Listenpreis zahlen. articles.title: NASA insider hints bigger UFO file release as declassification ramps up
articles.summary: Mike Gold, a former NASA UAP study member, says officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material beyond the previously released “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold told Ross Coultart on Newsnight that the National Archives are shifting toward declassifying material that remains classified and that bureaucracy is slowing the process. He argues that current disclosures focus too much on military and Earth‑based sightings and urges investigators to examine NASA’s space‑based archives, noting that no data from space assets has yet been released. Gold expects the forthcoming release to contain more sensitive material and believes it could be “even more interesting” than earlier tranches. Reddit users reacted to Gold’s comments, with some expressing optimism about the progress and others remaining skeptical after earlier releases lacked new evidence.

en.title: NASA insider hints bigger UFO file release as declassification ramps up
en.summary: Mike Gold, a former NASA UAP study member, says officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material beyond the previously released “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold told Ross Coultart on Newsnight that the National Archives are shifting toward declassifying material that remains classified and that bureaucracy is slowing the process. He argues that current disclosures focus too much on military and Earth‑based sightings and urges investigators to examine NASA’s space‑based archives, noting that no data from space assets has yet been released.

de.title: NASA-Insider deuten auf eine größere Veröffentlichung von UFO-Dateien hin, da die Freigabe voranschreitet
de.summary: Mike Gold, ein ehemaliges NASA-UAP-Studienmitglied, sagt, dass Beamte über die Freigabe von noch geheimem UFO-Material diskutieren, das über die zuvor veröffentlichten „Low-Hanging Fruits“ hinausgeht. Gold sagte Ross Coultart auf Newsnight, dass die Nationalarchive dazu übergehen, weiterhin geheim gehaltenes Material freizugeben, und dass die Bürokratie den Prozess verlangsame. Er argumentiert, dass sich die aktuellen Offenlegungen zu sehr auf militärische und erdbasierte Sichtungen konzentrieren, und fordert die Ermittler dringend auf, die weltraumgestützten Archive der NASA zu untersuchen, wobei er darauf hinweist, dass noch keine Daten von Weltraumanlagen veröffentlicht wurden.

articles.title: Trump UFO advisors have explanations for glowing orbs — except for one recent encounter
articles.summary: Trump administration science advisors are providing conventional explanations for some UFO sightings but say one recent encounter remains unexplained. Dr. Avi Loeb, head of the UAP Science Advisory Council, argues that many glowing orbs could be plasmas produced by powerful lasers, which can move supersonically without generating a sonic boom or fireball. Loeb notes that these laser‑based explanations cannot account for the October 2023 sighting of orange and red orbs near a secured military facility in the western United States, which were observed by federal intelligence officers. The All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) report, referenced by Loeb, describes an orange “mother orb” that launched smaller red orbs, remained stationary for hours, and exhibited coordinated horizontal motion and altitude changes, a behavior that does not match laser‑sustained plasma. Admiral Tim Gallaudet agrees that the “orbs launching orbs” case is a chief concern and cannot be explained by conventional means, while Loeb also considers ball lighting as a possibility but notes its brief duration compared to the hours‑long sighting.

de.title: Trumps UFO-Berater haben Erklärungen für leuchtende Kugeln – mit Ausnahme einer kürzlichen Begegnung
de.summary: Wissenschaftliche Berater der Trump-Administration liefern herkömmliche Erklärungen für einige UFO-Sichtungen, sagen jedoch, dass eine kürzliche Begegnung noch ungeklärt sei. Dr. Avi Loeb, Vorsitzender des UAP Science Advisory Council, argumentiert, dass viele leuchtende Kugeln Plasmen sein könnten, die von leistungsstarken Lasern erzeugt werden, die sich mit Überschall bewegen können, ohne einen Überschallknall oder Feuerball zu erzeugen.

articles.title: Exclusive: 2 SW Florida cities top 10 for UFO sightings
articles.summary: Naples and Cape Coral are ranked No. 6 and No. 9 respectively among Florida cities for UFO sightings according to data from the National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC). The NUFORC list, which tracks sightings submitted since 1995, shows Naples with 134 reports and Cape Coral with 117, while other Southwest Florida cities such as Captiva, Bonita Springs, and Fort Myers Beach have 22, 18, and 11 reports respectively. Naples Police Lt. Bryan McGinn said he has not received any service calls about UFO or UAP sightings, indicating that local authorities are not being notified of the reports. UFO experts note that about 5 % of sightings remain unexplained, a figure cited by journalist Leslie Kean, who has written on the subject and reported on a $22 million U.S. Department of Defense program investigating UFOs. Recent reports on NUFORC include a 2026 sighting in Port Charlotte of lights descending from the sky, a 2025 March report of a possible balloon in Naples, and a March 22, 2025 sighting of a black, shapeshifting object hovering over Green Cove Springs near Jacksonville. The article does not confirm whether any of these sightings were linked to rocket launches or drones, leaving the true nature of the objects uncertain.

de.summary: Nach Angaben des National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC) liegen Naples und Cape Coral in Bezug auf UFO-Sichtungen auf Platz 6 bzw. 9 unter den Städten Floridas.

articles.title: These 20 Tennessee cities report the most UFO sightings. Where yours ranks
articles.summary: Since the 2023 National Defense Authorization Act, U.S. reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs) have risen, with the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) reviewing over 1,000 cases but finding no evidence of extraterrestrial technology. UFO investigator Mick West, who has examined roughly 1,000 cases and analyzed about 100 in depth, advises a methodical approach that cross‑checks sightings with flight data, weather, ground cameras, and satellite imagery to rule out mundane explanations. West recommends using tools such as Invisor to extract metadata from videos, FlightRadar24 to view contemporaneous aircraft positions, and his own Sitrec software to reconstruct the scene with satellite data. AARO accepts UAP reports only from official sources like the military and FAA, while civilians can share their footage on databases such as Enigma and MUFON for community analysis. The article notes that while most anomalies have logical explanations, some remain unexplained and could potentially be the first true alien encounter, though this remains uncertain.

en.summary: Since the 2023 National Defense Authorization Act, U.S. reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs) have risen, with the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) reviewing over 1,000 cases but finding no evidence of extraterrestrial technology. UFO investigator Mick West, who has examined roughly 1,000 cases and analyzed about 100 in depth, advises a methodical approach that cross‑checks sightings with flight data, weather, ground cameras, and satellite imagery to rule out mundane explanations.

de.title: Diese 20 Städte in Tennessee melden die meisten UFO-Sichtungen. Wo Ihr Rang liegt
de.summary: Seit dem National Defense Authorization Act von 2023 sind in den USA die Meldungen über nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene (UAPs) gestiegen. Das All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) hat über 1.000 Fälle geprüft, aber keine Hinweise auf außerirdische Technologie gefunden. Der UFO-Ermittler Mick West, der rund 1.000 Fälle untersucht und etwa 100 eingehend analysiert hat, rät zu einem methodischen Ansatz, bei dem Sichtungen mit Flugdaten, Wetter, Bodenkameras und Satellitenbildern abgeglichen werden, um banale Erklärungen auszuschließen.

articles.title: Moon monoliths are Luis Elizondo's latest UFO bombshell
articles.summary: Luis Elizondo, a former Army counterintelligence special agent and long‑time UFO whistleblower, said that unreleased photographs of the Moon show monolithic structures with right‑angle cuts that he believes are artificial and may be released soon. He also described encounters with colored orbs—green, red, and blue—that he claims could be autonomous surveillance or weapon platforms, citing reports from the Pentagon’s former AATIP. The interview contains no new public evidence; the claims rely on photographs and reports that have not yet been made public. The Reddit UFO community reacted skeptically, demanding concrete proof rather than further announcements. Elizondo repeatedly used tentative language such as “appears to be” and noted that CIA, DIA, and NSA officials have concluded the objects are real and potentially threatening, but the existence of the alleged Moon structures remains unverified. He has previously claimed that a secret government program holds alien craft and that UAPs can disable nuclear missiles, but those claims are not substantiated in this interview.

en.title: Moon monoliths are Luis Elizondo's latest UFO bombshell
en.summary: Luis Elizondo, a former Army counterintelligence special agent and long‑time UFO whistleblower, said that unreleased photographs of the Moon show monolithic structures with right‑angle cuts that he believes are artificial and may be released soon. He also described encounters with colored orbs—green, red, and blue—that he claims could be autonomous surveillance or weapon platforms, citing reports from the Pentagon’s former AATIP. The interview contains no new public evidence; the claims rely on photographs and reports that have not yet been made public.

de.title: Mondmonolithen sind Luis Elizondos neueste UFO-Bombe
de.summary: Luis Elizondo, ein ehemaliger Spionageabwehr-Spezialagent der Armee und langjähriger UFO-Whistleblower, sagte, dass unveröffentlichte Fotos des Mondes monolithische Strukturen mit rechtwinkligen Schnitten zeigen, die seiner Meinung nach künstlich sind und möglicherweise bald veröffentlicht werden. Er beschrieb auch Begegnungen mit farbigen Kugeln – grün, rot und blau –, bei denen es sich seiner Meinung nach um autonome Überwachungs- oder Waffenplattformen handeln könnte, und berief sich dabei auf Berichte der früheren AATIP des Pentagons. Das Interview enthält keine neuen öffentlichen Beweise; Die Behauptungen stützen sich auf Fotos und Berichte, die noch nicht veröffentlicht wurden.

summaries.file-release-department-of-war-publishes: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.
summaries.pentagon-investigating-mysterious-uap-ev: Pentagon investigating mysterious UAP event reported by the Navy near Virginia’s coast Experts in the Pentagon’s All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office are investigating a report from the U. Navy involving roughly 100 mysterious airborne objects and watercraft that were recorded operating off the coast of Virginia. AARO’s Fiscal Year 2025 Consolidated Annual Report on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena, released this week, briefly discloses that modern military account of UAP swarming near a U. “A lack of timely and actionable sensor data continues to constrain AARO’s ability to resolve cases,” officials wrote in the 14-page review.
summaries.ufo-files-lawmaker-asks-fbi-cia-for-info: (NewsNation) — A member of the caucus in Congress responsible for investigating aerial phenomena has asked the FBI and CIA for help in probing an alleged UFO crash in southeast Brazil from three decades ago. Brazilian witnesses from January 1996 claimed at least one extraterrestrial survived the crash of a cigar-shaped craft and even received medical attention. The incident is sometimes referred to as the “Roswell of Brazil,” a reference to the alleged 1947 crash of a UFO in New Mexico. Last week, the transparency advocate sent letters to the FBI and CIA requesting any records they have about the Brazilian event.
summaries.ross-coulthart-pentagon-knows-a-lot-more: (NewsNation) — The Pentagon has declassified 40 additional UFO files containing information dating back to the 1940s. The files released on Friday include a conference between scientists at Los Alamos National Lab about green Fireballs sighted near the facility. “Something is taking a very close intelligence surveillance and reconnaissance interest in nuclear weapons and nuclear power,” said NewsNation Special Correspondent Ross Coulthart. The host of Reality Check spoke with Hena Doba on Sunday about the files and how the details about sightings near nuclear plants and weapons are intriguing. “One of them is over the Pantex nuclear plant in Amarillo, Texas,” said Coulthart.
summaries.program-nasa-s-changing-stance-on-ufos-f: NASA's relationship with UFOs has evolved alongside broader changes in public and government interest in unexplained sightings. Newsweek spoke with experts about how the public's and scientific community's views have evolved over time. As new UFO—now commonly referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs)—reports, official investigations and congressional hearings have brought the topic back into the spotlight, the agency has increasingly emphasized a scientific approach to studying the phenomenon. Former NASA official and UAP study team member Michael Gold recently argued that the topic was not always viewed through the same lens.
summaries.file-release-david-grusch-returns-to-cap: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels. Representatives Eric Burlison, Jared Moskowitz, Anna Paulina Luna, and Tim Burchett are expected to attend, while Kean and Fox will host the gathering. Grusch’s testimony has already moved the UAP topic from internet speculation to sworn congressional testimony, and the Capitol event aims to push for the review and public release of any existing classified records, subject to national‑security safeguards. The organizers argue that if such files exist, the issue shifts from public curiosity to whether intelligence agencies or contractors have operated beyond congressional oversight, and that secrecy cannot be a permanent shield for unlawful concealment or obstruction of Congress.
summaries.stream-disclosure-day-2026-online-at-hom: Steven Spielberg’s science‑fiction film *Disclosure Day* will be available for digital purchase on Prime Video beginning July 21, 2026, with a release time of 1:00 AM EDT. The film is offered as a UHD pre‑order for $29.99, and Amazon’s Pre‑order Price Guarantee ensures customers pay the lowest listed price between ordering and release. The cast includes Emily Blunt, Josh O’Connor, Colin Firth, Eve Hewson and Colman Domingo, and the screenplay is by David Koepp. After a theatrical run earlier in 2026 that earned $233 million worldwide against a $115 million budget, the movie has sparked widespread discussion among science‑fiction fans. The synopsis poses the question, “If you found out we weren’t alone, would that frighten you?” and describes a world changed by the revelation of extraterrestrial life. The article notes that further home‑media releases such as Blu‑ray, 4K UHD or streaming subscription availability will be announced if they become available.
summaries.file-release-nasa-insider-hints-bigger-u: Mike Gold, a former NASA UAP study member, says officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material beyond the previously released “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold told Ross Coultart on Newsnight that the National Archives are shifting toward declassifying material that remains classified and that bureaucracy is slowing the process. He argues that current disclosures focus too much on military and Earth‑based sightings and urges investigators to examine NASA’s space‑based archives, noting that no data from space assets has yet been released. Gold expects the forthcoming release to contain more sensitive material and believes it could be “even more interesting” than earlier tranches. Reddit users reacted to Gold’s comments, with some expressing optimism about the progress and others remaining skeptical after earlier releases lacked new evidence.
summaries.sighting-trump-ufo-advisors-have-explana: Trump administration science advisors are providing conventional explanations for some UFO sightings but say one recent encounter remains unexplained. Dr. Avi Loeb, head of the UAP Science Advisory Council, argues that many glowing orbs could be plasmas produced by powerful lasers, which can move supersonically without generating a sonic boom or fireball. Loeb notes that these laser‑based explanations cannot account for the October 2023 sighting of orange and red orbs near a secured military facility in the western United States, which were observed by federal intelligence officers. The All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) report, referenced by Loeb, describes an orange “mother orb” that launched smaller red orbs, remained stationary for hours, and exhibited coordinated horizontal motion and altitude changes, a behavior that does not match laser‑sustained plasma. Admiral Tim Gallaudet agrees that the “orbs launching orbs” case is a chief concern and cannot be explained by conventional means, while Loeb also considers ball lighting as a possibility but notes its brief duration compared to the hours‑long sighting.
summaries.program-exclusive-2-sw-florida-cities-to: Naples and Cape Coral are ranked No. 6 and No. 9 respectively among Florida cities for UFO sightings according to data from the National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC). The NUFORC list, which tracks sightings submitted since 1995, shows Naples with 134 reports and Cape Coral with 117, while other Southwest Florida cities such as Captiva, Bonita Springs, and Fort Myers Beach have 22, 18, and 11 reports respectively. Naples Police Lt. Bryan McGinn said he has not received any service calls about UFO or UAP sightings, indicating that local authorities are not being notified of the reports. UFO experts note that about 5 % of sightings remain unexplained, a figure cited by journalist Leslie Kean, who has written on the subject and reported on a $22 million U.S. Department of Defense program investigating UFOs. Recent reports on NUFORC include a 2026 sighting in Port Charlotte of lights descending from the sky, a 2025 March report of a possible balloon in Naples, and a March 22, 2025 sighting of a black, shapeshifting object hovering over Green Cove Springs near Jacksonville. The article does not confirm whether any of these sightings were linked to rocket launches or drones, leaving the true nature of the objects uncertain.

AARO accepts UAP reports only from official sources like the military and FAA, while civilians can share their footage on databases such as Enigma and MUFON for community analysis. The article notes that while most anomalies have logical explanations, some remain unexplained and could potentially be the first true alien encounter, though this remains uncertain.

The Reddit UFO community reacted skeptically, demanding concrete proof rather than further announcements. en.title: Department of War Publishes Fourth Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die vierte Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

Navy involving roughly 100 mysterious airborne objects and watercraft that were recorded operating off the coast of Virginia.

de.title: Pentagon untersucht mysteriöses UAP-Ereignis, das von der Marine nahe der Küste Virginias gemeldet wurde
de.summary: Pentagon untersucht mysteriöses UAP-Ereignis, das von der Marine nahe der Küste Virginias gemeldet wurde. Experten im All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office des Pentagons untersuchen einen Bericht der US-Marine, in dem es um etwa 100 mysteriöse Flugobjekte und Wasserfahrzeuge geht, die vor der Küste Virginias im Einsatz waren.

en.title: UFO files: Lawmaker asks FBI, CIA for info on Brazil’s Roswell moment
en.summary: (NewsNation) — A member of the caucus in Congress responsible for investigating aerial phenomena has asked the FBI and CIA for help in probing an alleged UFO crash in southeast Brazil from three decades ago. Brazilian witnesses from January 1996 claimed at least one extraterrestrial survived the crash of a cigar-shaped craft and even received medical attention. The incident is sometimes referred to as the “Roswell of Brazil,” a reference to the alleged 1947 crash of a UFO in New Mexico.

de.title: UFO-Akten: Gesetzgeber bittet FBI und CIA um Informationen zum brasilianischen Roswell-Moment
de.summary: (NewsNation) – Ein Mitglied des Caucus im Kongress, das für die Untersuchung von Luftphänomenen zuständig ist, hat das FBI und die CIA um Hilfe bei der Untersuchung eines mutmaßlichen UFO-Absturzes im Südosten Brasiliens vor drei Jahrzehnten gebeten. Brasilianische Zeugen behaupteten im Januar 1996, mindestens ein Außerirdischer habe den Absturz eines zigarrenförmigen Raumschiffs überlebt und sei sogar medizinisch versorgt worden. Der Vorfall wird manchmal als „Roswell von Brasilien“ bezeichnet, eine Anspielung auf den angeblichen Absturz eines UFOs im Jahr 1947 in New Mexico.

en.title: Ross Coulthart: Pentagon knows ‘a lot more’ about UAP than it admits
en.summary: (NewsNation) — The Pentagon has declassified 40 additional UFO files containing information dating back to the 1940s. The files released on Friday include a conference between scientists at Los Alamos National Lab about green Fireballs sighted near the facility. “Something is taking a very close intelligence surveillance and reconnaissance interest in nuclear weapons and nuclear power,” said NewsNation Special Correspondent Ross Coulthart.

de.title: Ross Coulthart: Das Pentagon weiß „viel mehr“ über UAP, als es zugibt
de.summary: (NewsNation) – Das Pentagon hat 40 weitere UFO-Dateien freigegeben, die Informationen aus den 1940er Jahren enthalten. Zu den am Freitag veröffentlichten Akten gehört eine Konferenz zwischen Wissenschaftlern des Los Alamos National Lab über grüne Feuerbälle, die in der Nähe der Anlage gesichtet wurden. „Etwas nimmt ein sehr starkes nachrichtendienstliches Überwachungs- und Aufklärungsinteresse an Atomwaffen und Atomkraft an“, sagte NewsNation-Sonderkorrespondent Ross Coulthart.

en.title: NASA’s Changing Stance on UFOs: Full Timeline
en.summary: NASA's relationship with UFOs has evolved alongside broader changes in public and government interest in unexplained sightings. Newsweek spoke with experts about how the public's and scientific community's views have evolved over time. As new UFO—now commonly referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs)—reports, official investigations and congressional hearings have brought the topic back into the spotlight, the agency has increasingly emphasized a scientific approach to studying the phenomenon.

de.title: Die sich ändernde Haltung der NASA zu UFOs: Vollständige Zeitleiste
de.summary: Die Beziehung der NASA zu UFOs hat sich im Zuge umfassenderer Veränderungen im öffentlichen und staatlichen Interesse an ungeklärten Sichtungen weiterentwickelt. Newsweek sprach mit Experten darüber, wie sich die Ansichten der Öffentlichkeit und der wissenschaftlichen Gemeinschaft im Laufe der Zeit entwickelt haben. Da neue UFO-Berichte, offizielle Untersuchungen und Anhörungen vor dem Kongress das Thema wieder ins Rampenlicht gerückt haben – heute allgemein als nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene (UAPs) bezeichnet – hat die Agentur zunehmend Wert auf einen wissenschaftlichen Ansatz bei der Untersuchung des Phänomens gelegt.

en.title: David Grusch Returns To Capitol Steps As UFO Disclosure Fight Moves From Testimony To Action
en.summary: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels.

de.title: David Grusch kehrt zu den Stufen des Kapitols zurück, während der UFO-Enthüllungskampf von der Aussage zur Tat übergeht
de.summary: Am 9. Juni 2026 soll der ehemalige Geheimdienstoffizier David Grusch auf den Stufen des US-Kapitols mit dem Filmemacher James Fox, dem Autor Leslie Kean und mehreren überparteilichen Vertretern sprechen, um die Freigabe von UFO- und UAP-Dateien zu fordern. Die Veranstaltung ist als Aufruf zur Freigabe, einem stärkeren Whistleblower-Schutz und einer neuen Offenlegungsgesetzgebung angelegt, wobei Grusch plant, Beweise für nichtmenschliche Geheimdienste zu diskutieren, die er angeblich über geheime Kanäle eingesehen hat.

de.title: Streamen Sie Disclosure Day (2026) ab nächster Woche online zu Hause!

en.title: NASA insider hints bigger UFO file release as declassification ramps up
en.summary: Mike Gold, a former NASA UAP study member, says officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material beyond the previously released “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold told Ross Coultart on Newsnight that the National Archives are shifting toward declassifying material that remains classified and that bureaucracy is slowing the process. He argues that current disclosures focus too much on military and Earth‑based sightings and urges investigators to examine NASA’s space‑based archives, noting that no data from space assets has yet been released.

de.title: NASA-Insider deuten auf eine größere Veröffentlichung von UFO-Dateien hin, da die Freigabe voranschreitet
de.summary: Mike Gold, ein ehemaliges NASA-UAP-Studienmitglied, sagt, dass Beamte über die Freigabe von noch geheimem UFO-Material diskutieren, das über die zuvor veröffentlichten „Low-Hanging Fruits“ hinausgeht. Gold sagte Ross Coultart auf Newsnight, dass die Nationalarchive dazu übergehen, weiterhin geheim gehaltenes Material freizugeben, und dass die Bürokratie den Prozess verlangsame. Er argumentiert, dass sich die aktuellen Offenlegungen zu sehr auf militärische und erdbasierte Sichtungen konzentrieren, und fordert die Ermittler dringend auf, die weltraumgestützten Archive der NASA zu untersuchen, wobei er darauf hinweist, dass noch keine Daten von Weltraumanlagen veröffentlicht wurden.

de.title: Trumps UFO-Berater haben Erklärungen für leuchtende Kugeln – mit Ausnahme einer kürzlichen Begegnung
de.summary: Wissenschaftliche Berater der Trump-Administration liefern herkömmliche Erklärungen für einige UFO-Sichtungen, sagen jedoch, dass eine kürzliche Begegnung noch ungeklärt sei. Dr. Avi Loeb, Vorsitzender des UAP Science Advisory Council, argumentiert, dass viele leuchtende Kugeln Plasmen sein könnten, die von leistungsstarken Lasern erzeugt werden, die sich mit Überschall bewegen können, ohne einen Überschallknall oder Feuerball zu erzeugen.

de.summary: Nach Angaben des National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC) liegen Naples und Cape Coral in Bezug auf UFO-Sichtungen auf Platz 6 bzw. 9 unter den Städten Floridas.

en.title: These 20 Tennessee cities report the most UFO sightings. Where yours ranks
en.summary: Since the 2023 National Defense Authorization Act, U.S. reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs) have risen, with the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) reviewing over 1,000 cases but finding no evidence of extraterrestrial technology. UFO investigator Mick West, who has examined roughly 1,000 cases and analyzed about 100 in depth, advises a methodical approach that cross‑checks sightings with flight data, weather, ground cameras, and satellite imagery to rule out mundane explanations.

de.title: Diese 20 Städte in Tennessee melden die meisten UFO-Sichtungen. Wo Ihr Rang liegt
de.summary: Seit dem National Defense Authorization Act von 2023 sind in den USA die Meldungen über nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene (UAPs) gestiegen. Das All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) hat über 1.000 Fälle geprüft, aber keine Hinweise auf außerirdische Technologie gefunden. Der UFO-Ermittler Mick West, der rund 1.000 Fälle untersucht und etwa 100 eingehend analysiert hat, rät zu einem methodischen Ansatz, bei dem Sichtungen mit Flugdaten, Wetter, Bodenkameras und Satellitenbildern abgeglichen werden, um banale Erklärungen auszuschließen.

en.title: Moon monoliths are Luis Elizondo's latest UFO bombshell
en.summary: Luis Elizondo, a former Army counterintelligence special agent and long‑time UFO whistleblower, said that unreleased photographs of the Moon show monolithic structures with right‑angle cuts that he believes are artificial and may be released soon. He also described encounters with colored orbs—green, red, and blue—that he claims could be autonomous surveillance or weapon platforms, citing reports from the Pentagon’s former AATIP. The interview contains no new public evidence; the claims rely on photographs and reports that have not yet been made public.

de.title: Mondmonolithen sind Luis Elizondos neueste UFO-Bombe
de.summary: Luis Elizondo, ein ehemaliger Spionageabwehr-Spezialagent der Armee und langjähriger UFO-Whistleblower, sagte, dass unveröffentlichte Fotos des Mondes monolithische Strukturen mit rechtwinkligen Schnitten zeigen, die seiner Meinung nach künstlich sind und möglicherweise bald veröffentlicht werden. Er beschrieb auch Begegnungen mit farbigen Kugeln – grün, rot und blau –, bei denen es sich seiner Meinung nach um autonome Überwachungs- oder Waffenplattformen handeln könnte, und berief sich dabei auf Berichte der früheren AATIP des Pentagons. Das Interview enthält keine neuen öffentlichen Beweise; Die Behauptungen stützen sich auf Fotos und Berichte, die noch nicht veröffentlicht wurden.

translationMeta.summary: compact app translation only; source summaries are not overwritten
translationMeta.target: de for English articles, en for German articles
